<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Adding a ChatGPT button after the copy prompt in `layouts/notes/random-two.html` and `random-three.html`, matching the blog layout structure.

Also adding the `sendPromptToAI` JavaScript logic to both `assets/js/notes-random-two.js` and `assets/js/notes-random-three.js`.

The notes pages currently pass only a URLs array in their JSON config, unlike the blog which includes aiChatUrl. I'm deciding whether to add that URL to the JSON or hardcode a fallback in JavaScript.

I'll add the `aiChatUrl` to the JSON config in the templates, matching the blog pattern. The JavaScript will be updated to parse both the old array format and the new object format.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Added the **Open in ChatGPT** button everywhere there’s a copy-prompt control. The blog partial already had it; the two notes pages were missing it.

**Updated templates** (`random-two.html`, `random-three.html`):
- Wrapped both buttons in the same `notes-random-two-prompt-actions` flex container used on blog posts
- Added `data-random-two-send-prompt` / `data-random-three-send-prompt` after Copy prompt

**Updated JS** (`notes-random-two.js`, `notes-random-three.js`):
- Same behavior as the blog script: prefills ChatGPT when the URL stays under 1800 chars; otherwise copies the prompt and opens ChatGPT for manual paste
- Buttons only work once notes are loaded (not on “Loading notes…” or placeholder text)

All three random-notes surfaces now have **Copy prompt** → **Open in ChatGPT** in that order.
